name: Assist & Attack
description: One-button engage for melee: assists the main assist, waits a beat for the target to land, then turns autoattack on. The single most important macro in the game for staying on the right kill target.
tips: Replace Tankname with your main assist. The pause fires AFTER /assist (comma semantics), so the new target is locked before /attack. Raise to /pause 10-20 to give the tank time to build aggro before you swing.

name: Assist Check (No Attack)
description: Caster-safe assist: acquires the main assist's target and announces it to group without turning melee on. Use before every nuke, mez, or debuff.
tips: %t evaluates after the assist, so the announce names the mob you actually targeted -- a built-in sanity check against nuking the mez target.

name: Camp Check
description: Cons the current target and stamps your coordinates and game time into chat (and your log file). Standard camp-check and respawn-timing button.
tips: With /log on, every press timestamps the camp state in your log -- useful data for a log-parsing companion app.

name: Corpse Drag
description: Hold-and-run corpse dragger: tugs your (or a consented) corpse toward you every second for five seconds per press.
tips: /corpse only works within about 50 feet of the corpse. To drag someone else's corpse they must /consent you first.

name: Double Corpse Drag
description: Leapfrogs two corpses at once by alternating target and drag. The wipe-recovery workhorse.
tips: Replace Alice and Bob with the actual names; both players must /consent you. Spam the button while running to the graveyard or zone line.

name: Consent Group
lines: /g Consented -- Dragger can pull my corpse
description: Grants a named player permission to drag your corpse and tells the group it is done. Make one per regular corpse-dragger in your crew.
tips: Revoke later with /deny Name. Consent before risky pulls, not after you are dead and running back naked.

name: Incoming!
description: Puller announce: names the mob you are dragging home so the group knows what is coming. Press while the pull mob is targeted.
tips: Add a second line like '/g Sit NOW to grab aggro-free med time' or '/g CASTERS STAND' to fit your group's habits. Keep it one line if you pull with sows on -- fast fingers matter.

name: Train Warning
description: Zone-wide train apology plus a /yell, which broadcasts your direction to nearby players so they know which way death is coming from.
tips: The classic courtesy button. Do not be the person who trains silently.

name: Auto-Follow
description: Targets your group's runner and locks on auto-follow. Re-press whenever follow breaks on turns or zone lines.
tips: Straight from Almar's guide. Follow only works on group members; any movement key breaks it. Swap Leadername per session.

name: AFK Toggle
description: Flags you AFK with an auto-reply to tells and sits you down. Press again to clear AFK when you return.
tips: Pair with a 'Back' macro: /afk then /g Back! Pet classes: add /pet guard here so the pet does not wander.

name: Who's That? (GM/Anon Check)
description: Reveals a targeted player's /who line and guild affiliation even if they are anon or roleplay-flagged. Handy before group invites or when checking a suspicious name.
tips: From the P99 'useful hotkeys' thread -- veterans keep this bound at all times.

name: Loot Roll Call
lines: /g Rolling for the drop -- /random 100, highest wins
description: Announces the roll rules and immediately rolls 0-100 in front of the group. Everyone else presses their own /random 100.
tips: /random alone rolls 0-100; /random 333 500 rolls a custom range for tie-breaks.

name: Bind Wound Chain
description: Chains five Bind Wound attempts with the ~10s re-bind delay between each. Free HP between pulls and passive skill-ups.
tips: Set 3 to whatever Ability-tab slot holds Bind Wound (slots 1-6 on the classic A tab). Requires bandages in inventory; taking a hit or moving interrupts. Target yourself or a hurt groupmate.

name: Fishing Loop
description: Casts your line twice per press, auto-bagging whatever you reel in. Face water with a fishing pole equipped and bait in inventory.
tips: P99 wiki verbatim. The 13s pause covers the reel-in time; the /autoinventory stops fish from sitting on your cursor.

name: Forage & Stash
description: Forages and auto-bags the result (two autoinventory calls in case of stacked cursor items), then paints a 60s visual cooldown on the button.
tips: /timer is a Legends-only visual cooldown (tenths of a second, cosmetic only) -- delete that line on classic emulators. Any race/class with the Forage skill can use this; set 2 to your Forage slot.

name: Session Log Start
description: Turns on the text log file and stamps location, game time, and /played into it. Press at the start of every session -- log parsers (like Legends Companion) need /log on to see anything.
tips: Logging persists per character. /outputfile inventory Filename.txt is a companion trick for exporting your inventory to a file.

name: Insta-Camp
description: Flags AFK, sits, and starts the 30-second camp timer in one press.
tips: Use /camp desktop to exit straight to Windows. Standing or taking damage cancels the camp.

name: Hide Corpses
description: Hides every corpse except your group's -- massive clutter and lag reduction at raid wipes and heavily-farmed camps.
tips: /hidecorpse none restores visibility; /hidecorpse looted hides corpses as you loot them. Newly spawned corpses still appear until you re-press.

name: Taunt & Report
lines: /g Taunting %t -- feed me a second to lock aggro
description: Fires Taunt from the Combat tab and tells the group who you are peeling. Spam it when a caster catches aggro.
tips: Classic /doability numbering: 1-6 = Abilities tab, 7-10 = Combat tab; put Taunt in slot 7. Drop the announce line if you prefer a silent spam button.

name: Kick + Attack Safety
description: Guarantees autoattack is on and fires Kick (or Flying Kick) in one press. Spam it as your main melee button and you will never stand there not swinging.
tips: Set 8 to your kick's Combat-tab slot. The /attack on guard is the classic fix for 'why am I not attacking' -- it never toggles attack off.

name: Shield Bash
description: Autoattack guard plus Bash. Time it against enemy casters -- a landed bash interrupts their cast.
tips: Requires a shield equipped. Put Bash in Combat slot 9 and spam; it simply fails silently while on cooldown.

name: Backstab Spam
description: The rogue bread-and-butter: keeps autoattack on and fires Backstab every time it refreshes.
tips: Needs a piercing weapon in the main hand and you must be behind the target. Spam freely -- failed attempts cost nothing.

name: Rogue Evade
description: The aggro-dump: flips attack off, Hides mid-combat (evade), and resumes attacking. Use whenever you rip aggro off the tank.
tips: Set 3 to your Hide slot. Evade only works while the mob is engaged on someone else and you are stationary for the instant of the Hide. Repeat every big backstab chain.

name: Monk FD Drop
description: Instant feign death: kills autoattack (a swing cancels FD) then flops. Kept to two lines so nothing can delay the feign.
tips: P99 monk consensus: keep the FD button minimal -- no pauses, no announces. Put Feign Death in Ability slot 1 on every monk you make.

name: Monk FD Split
description: The pull-splitting sequence: feign, wait 10 seconds while the extra mobs path home, Sneak, then stand. Sneak-standing keeps mobs behind you from re-aggroing, so only the near mob comes back.
tips: Set 1=Feign Death, 2=Sneak. Tune the standalone /pause to the camp's pathing -- longer for deep splits. Stand only when the mob you want is closest; abort by not pressing at all and flopping manually.

name: Mend & Report
description: Fires Mend and tells the healer it is spent, so they know your self-heal is down.
tips: Mend can critically fail at low skill and hurt you instead -- do not lean on it below 50% HP at low levels.

name: Ranger Bow Assist
description: Assists the tank and opens up with auto-firing ranged attacks -- perfect while mobs are inbound or when you are told to stay at range.
tips: /autofire exists in EQ Legends; on strict classic-era servers you hold the ranged-attack key instead. Turn it off (/autofire off) before closing to melee, and use /bandolier to swap to melee weapons.

name: Shield Ally
lines: /g Shielding Clericname -- I eat their hits, keep me up
description: Warrior /shield: intercepts a chunk of incoming damage for the named player for a short duration. Use on the CH cleric or a training caster.
tips: You must stay in melee range of the shielded player for the duration. Syntax per the Legends wiki is /shield <playername>.

name: Defensive Disc Call
lines: /rs DEFENSIVE UP -- max heals for the duration, call when it drops
description: Pops a mitigation discipline and tells the raid so healers adjust. The announce is the whole point -- silent discs waste healer mana.
tips: /disc <name> performs the named discipline (confirmed in Legends). Which discs exist at Legends' level-50 cap may differ from classic (Defensive was 55 in old EQ) -- swap in whatever your class has.

name: Berserker Frenzy Spam
description: Berserker main button: autoattack guard plus Frenzy every time it refreshes.
tips: Berserker is new to Legends (never existed in classic), so slot numbers and ability names come from the Legends Actions window -- put Frenzy in a Combat slot and spam. Add /autofire on for throwing-axe openers.

name: Weapon Set Swap
description: Swaps to a saved weapon set in one press -- bow to melee, epic to bash shield, proc weapons to pacify-safe fists.
tips: Create sets first with /bandolier add <SetName> while holding the gear. Legends feature: classic-era EQ had no bandolier, swaps were manual inventory drags.

name: CH Rotation Call
description: Raid Complete Heal chain: announce your CH with the target's name, double-tap the cast to eat one fizzle, ride out the 10s cast, then call the next cleric in rotation.
tips: The second /cast 1 only fires if the first fizzled (you cannot double-cast mid-cast). Replace NextCleric and tune the pause to your chain interval -- tighter chains use a shorter pause so the call lands early. Classic convention writes it 'CH -- %t -- 007' with each cleric's number.

name: Complete Heal Announce
description: Group CH: announces the heal target, casts, waits out the full cast, and sits to med. The announce stops the tank from wasting a potion and the group from panicking.
tips: P99 forum verbatim. Pause 110 covers the 10s cast plus recovery; shorten if you want to move sooner and sit manually.

name: Mez -- DO NOT TOUCH
lines: /g Mezzing %t << DO NOT TOUCH -- no DoTs, no pets, no AEs
description: The mez announce every enchanter runs: names the add, warns the group off it, then casts. Any damage breaks mez, so the warning IS the crowd control.
tips: Announce first so the mob's name is spoken even if it dies or resists. Bards: point the cast line at your mez song gem. Pair with a Tash macro ('/g Tashing %t' + /cast 2) for resist fights.

name: Charm Warning
lines: /g Charming %t -- if my pet turns on me it BROKE: assist and kill it, do NOT scatter
description: Announces the charm target and the break plan before casting. Charm is the strongest and most dangerous spell in classic -- the group must know the drill in advance.
tips: Druids charm animals only, necros undead only. Keep the mob debuffed (Tash/Malo) and always have the break plan macro'd, not typed in a panic.

name: CHARM BROKE -- Remez
lines: /g CHARM BROKE -- remezzing, get OFF it
description: Panic button for charm breaks: warns the group and mezzes your ex-pet in one press.
tips: Bind a 'cycle nearest NPC' key next to this -- the broken pet is usually the closest NPC to you. Rebuff rune on yourself after it is locked down.

name: Root Park
lines: /g Rooting %t -- PARKED, stay out of its melee range
description: Roots an add in place and tells the group it is parked. Cheap crowd control for camps without an enchanter.
tips: Root can break on any damage tick -- re-announce on breaks. Park mobs away from the med circle; rooted mobs still melee whatever stands next to them.

name: Snare Call
lines: /g %t SNARED -- no runners, burn it down
description: Snares the kill target and announces it, so the group knows it cannot flee at low HP and bring friends.
tips: Snare first, then DoT/nuke -- classic pulling discipline. Necro/SK Darkness lines double as aggro tools; announce prevents double-snaring.

name: Evac Countdown
lines: /rs EVAC IN 5 SECONDS -- stack on me NOW
description: The camp-saver: five-second warning, then casts Evacuate/Succor. Only group members in range go -- the countdown is what saves lives.
tips: Tune the standalone pause to your group's discipline (raise to /pause 100 for a 10s warning). Keep evac memmed in the same gem slot forever so the macro never lies.

name: Gate Out
lines: /g Gating -- see you at my bind, safe hunting
description: Announces and casts Gate so the group is not left wondering where their healer went.
tips: Keep Gate in the same gem slot on every spell set. For emergencies make a no-announce copy -- one line, /cast 8.

name: OOM Call
description: Tells the puller you are out of mana before the next pull, not during it.
tips: Classic and Legends have no mana variable for macros -- the 'Mana: 47%' convention is typed by hand (or tracked by a log companion). Make LOW MANA (30%) and OOM variants.

name: Mana Check
lines: /g MANA CHECK -- casters reply with your %
description: Group-leader button: polls caster mana between pulls. The replies pace the puller.
tips: Raid version: swap /g for /rs. Companion apps can automate the replies by watching the log for this line.

name: Assist Nuke
description: Assist, announce, nuke -- with a one-second pause after the assist so the target resolves and you never nuke the mez target.
tips: Raise the pause to /pause 30-50 early in fights to let the tank build aggro; classic wizard aggro is unforgiving. Swap gem numbers for your nuke ladder.

name: Caster Feign Death
description: Calls the pet off and casts Feign Death to dump aggro. The pet line matters -- an attacking pet keeps you on the hate list.
tips: Keep FD in a fixed gem slot. After the camp settles, stand and rebuff (necros: re-lich, re-darkness the split target). SKs get FD as a spell later than monks get the skill.

name: Pet Assist
description: Assists the tank, sends the pet at that target, and announces which mob the pet is eating. Prevents the classic disaster of the pet peeling the mez target.
tips: %m speaks your pet's name. The pause fires after the assist so /pet attack goes at the right mob. Never send pets before the tank has aggro.

name: Pet Recall
description: Yanks the pet off its target and puts it back on follow. Press early and often when a pull goes wrong.
tips: P99 wiki verbatim. Pet commands will not register while you are mid-cast -- press again after your cast finishes if the pet ignores you.

name: Pet Switch Target
description: Recalls the pet and immediately re-sends it at your current target -- the clean way to retarget a pet mid-fight.
tips: From the P99 enchanter macro thread. Target the new mob first (or lead with /assist Tankname on line 1).

name: Park Pet
description: Stops the pet and anchors it at its current spot -- pre-pull discipline so it cannot leash onto inbound mobs.
tips: Legends also supports /pet hold. Re-park after every fight; a following pet is an accidental-aggro machine in dungeons.

name: Mage Pet Arsenal
description: Summons two pet weapons (dagger/sword line), bagging each, then announces the handoff. Give both to the pet for the damage and proc upgrade.
tips: Tune /pause to the summon's cast time plus recovery (60 = 6s). You still hand weapons to the pet manually via trade. Rebuild after every pet death -- an armed mage pet is a different class.

name: Canni Dance
description: The shaman engine: stand, Cannibalize HP into mana, sit back down to med. Spam between every pull.
tips: P99 wiki verbatim; a popular variant is '/cast 7' + '/pause 24' + '/sit' -- tune to your Canni rank's cast time. Watch your HP and keep a heal ready; do not canni during AE fights.

name: Heal Incoming
description: Fast-heal announce: tells the group (and the target) that help is casting, so melee do not disengage and the tank does not burn cooldowns.
tips: Make one per heal rank in different gems. The announce doubles as an anti-overheal signal to a second healer.

name: Rez Announce
lines: /g Rezzing %t -- take it, then loot your corpse before it rots
description: Targets a corpse, announces the rez, and casts. %t speaks the corpse's name so the right player releases and takes it.
tips: For raids swap /g for /shout or a /tell to the dead player's name. State the rez percent in the text if you carry multiple ranks ('96% rez inc').

name: Buff Tell Reply
description: Answers a buff-beg in one press: targets whoever last sent you a tell (/rt), tells them to hold still (/tt goes to your target), and casts.
tips: From P99 raid-cleric practice. /rt and /tt are on the P99 command list; verify they exist in Legends before shipping as default. Swap the gem per buff (Clarity, SoW, Temperance...).

name: Port Taxi Call
lines: /ooc PORTING out shortly -- /tell me for a spot, donations appreciated
description: The classic port-taxi advertisement. Press in the commons tunnel or at the spires and watch the tells roll in.
tips: Keep one per destination ('Porting to NK/Ro/Butcher'). Pair with the Port Cast Warning macro when the group is assembled.

name: Port Cast Warning
lines: /g PORT INC -- click YES on the box or wave goodbye
description: Warns the group to accept the translocate/port confirm, waits four seconds, then casts the port.
tips: Group ports only take group members in range -- the pause is your straggler grace period. Tune it per group.

name: Main Assist Call
lines: /rs MAIN ASSIST: %t -- make an /assist hotkey on this name NOW
/g All damage through the MA -- /assist before you swing
description: Raid-leader button: target the MA player and press. Names the main assist to the raid and reminds everyone to build their assist hotkey.
tips: Legends also supports /assist main if the raid tool designates an MA -- mention it in the announce if your raid uses it.

name: CC Assignments
lines: /rs CC: I handle adds -- kill MY TARGET LAST, no AEs or pets without my GO
description: Pre-fight crowd-control contract with the raid. One press before every multi-pull event saves three broken mezzes.
tips: Customize per event ('mez order: caster adds first'). Repeat it after wipes -- new joiners never saw the first one.

name: Loot Rules
lines: /rs LOOT: need-before-greed -- /random 100 on contested drops, tells to the loot master for rares
description: Raid/group loot-rules announce. Stating rules before the first kill prevents every argument after it.
tips: Make group (/g) and raid (/rs) versions. Turn on /autosplit at group formation so coin never needs discussing.

name: Pull Timer
lines: /g Camp ready? Pulling in 10 -- med up and call OOM now
description: Puller courtesy clock: ten-second warning, then the commit call. Gives casters a fair chance to object before the mob is inbound.
tips: The standalone /pause 100 (10s) runs before line 3 fires. Cancel by just... not pulling; the macro only talks.

name: Bard Melody
description: Auto-twists the songs in gems 1-4 in order, repeating until stopped. The Legends bard's default combat state.
tips: Legends-only: classic-era EQ had NO /melody -- bards twisted by hand. /stopsong halts; /queuemelody <song> arranges by recast priority. Make one melody macro per situation (pull, burn, med).

name: Clicky Item
description: Activates an item's effect from a macro by name or inventory slot -- clicky buffs, epic effects, instrument swaps.
tips: Legends-only command (/useitem <slot> or /useitem "<name>"); classic-era required opening inventory and right-clicking. Swap the item name for your clicky of choice.

name: Feign Death Skill-Up
description: Downtime FD skill trainer: flop, sit (which stands you up from feign), flop again. Run it while medding at camp to cap Feign Death skill.
tips: P99 wiki verbatim (slot 1 = FD). Only do this at a safe camp -- a failed feign at the wrong moment says your name to the whole room.

name: Casting Skill Grinder
description: Chain-casts gem 1 five times per press -- the standard way to grind up a new specialization or casting skill on a cheap spell.
tips: P99 wiki verbatim. Mem the cheapest spell of the school you are skilling and sit-med between presses. Tune pause to cast time + recovery.
